name: Dentate Gyrus Neuroblast (Type-3 Progenitor)
morphology_summary: Migratory precursor cells in the SGZ; migrate only a few micrometers from SGZ toward the inner granule cell layer. At the type-2b stage they co-express Nestin and DCX; at the type-3 stage they are DCX+ only. They are proliferative (Ki67+) and represent the last mitotic stage before postmitotic differentiation.
ephys_summary: Not synaptically integrated; precursor electrophysiological properties not characterized in detail in this report.
subtypes_noted: The developmental sequence distinguishes type-2a (Nestin+/Sox2+), type-2b (Nestin+/DCX+), and type-3 (DCX+) stages. All are transit amplifying progenitors with progressively increasing neuronal commitment. A transcription factor cascade (Pax6 > Ngn2 > Tbr2 > NeuroD > Tbr1) marks stage transitions, with Tbr2 specifically marking intermediate progenitor cells.

name: Dentate Gyrus Immature Granule Neuron
morphology_summary: Postmitotic neurons (stage 5) that have migrated from SGZ into the inner granule cell layer. Axonal projections reach the CA3 area by 10-11 days, with first spines forming at 15-16 days. Peak spine growth occurs during the first 3-4 weeks, but further structural modifications continue for months. They receive glutamatergic afferents from entorhinal cortex and project axons to inhibitory interneurons and pyramidal cells of area CA3.
ephys_summary: Synaptically integrating; receive glutamatergic input from entorhinal cortex. Morphological maturation is differentially affected by age and experience (e.g., housing conditions). The timeline for full synaptic integration extends over weeks to months.
subtypes_noted: No distinct subtypes described. Represents a transitional maturational stage (stage 5 in the Micheli et al. classification) between DCX+ neuroblasts and fully mature granule neurons. Tis21 expression reappears during this maturation phase, potentially related to functional integration.

name: Dentate Gyrus Mature Granule Neuron
morphology_summary: Terminally differentiated granule neurons (stage 6) populating the granule cell layer. Fully elaborated dendritic arbor and complete axonal projections to CA3. Receive primary glutamatergic afferents from entorhinal cortex and project mossy fiber axons to inhibitory interneurons and pyramidal cells of area CA3.
ephys_summary: Fully synaptically integrated; functionally mature with established input from entorhinal cortex and output to CA3. Tis21 expression decreases in mature granule cells compared to the maturing stage.
subtypes_noted: No subtypes discussed in this report. The mature granule neuron is described as the terminal differentiation endpoint of the adult neurogenesis lineage in the dentate gyrus.

name: Piriform Cortex Layer II Immature Neuron
markers_mentioned: A3 subunit of cyclic nucleotide-gated channel
morphology_summary: Immature neurons in layer II of the piriform cortex classified by morphology into (1) tangled cells, very small cells with very short and intricate processes; (2) complex cells, more mature, displaying axon initial segments (AIS) and typical apical dendrites extending into layer I. Additional transitional forms include semilunar-pyramidal transitional neurons and scarce cells resembling semilunar, pyramidal, and fusiform neurons. Most cells lack synaptic contacts and are covered by astroglial lamellae.
ephys_summary: Cells fail to express cellular activity markers such as c-Fos or Arc, and lack NMDA or glucocorticoid receptors, consistent with a prolonged non-integrated immature state. A subpopulation is associated with synaptic plasticity and progressively resumes maturation into glutamatergic (TBR1+, CaMKII+) neurons.
subtypes_noted: Two main morphological subtypes: tangled cells (least mature, small, intricate short processes) and complex cells (more mature, with AIS and apical dendrites in layer I). Additional transitional forms: semilunar-pyramidal transitional neurons; scarce cells resembling semilunar, pyramidal, and fusiform neurons.

name: Olfactory Bulb Neuroblast / Immature Interneuron
morphology_summary: Type A neuroblasts have the ultrastructure of migrating neuronal precursors, arranged as chains parallel to the walls of the lateral ventricle and ensheathed by astrocytes (Type B1/B2). They migrate via chain migration through the RMS to the olfactory bulb (3-8 mm), then leave the RMS and migrate radially toward the glomeruli where they differentiate.
ephys_summary: Synaptic integration occurs around 21 days after neuroblast generation; at 10 days immature neurons are not yet synaptically integrated. Differentiated interneurons provide lateral inhibition: periglomerular neurons inhibit between individual glomeruli, while granule cells provide lateral inhibition between mitral and tufted cells.
subtypes_noted: SVZ lineage passes through type B (GFAP+ astrocyte-like NSCs), type C (actively dividing transit-amplifying progenitors, nestin+), and type A (migrating neuroblasts; PSA-NCAM+/TuJ1+/nestin+ but GFAP-/vimentin-). Terminal fates in the OB split into two classical subtypes: GABAergic granule neurons in the granule cell layer (majority) and GABAergic periglomerular neurons in the glomerular layer.

name: Striatum Immature Neuron (SVZ-derived)
morphology_summary: BrdU+/DCX+ cells are detected within the striatum, consistent with migrating neuroblasts at various stages of migration and early differentiation from the SVZ. No detailed morphological maturation stages are described for this region in the report.
ephys_summary: No electrophysiological characterization is described for striatal immature neurons in the report; adult striatal neurogenesis is described as quantitatively limited and controversial, with few immature neurons successfully integrating.
subtypes_noted: No subtypes explicitly described; cells are presumed to be inhibitory interneurons by analogy with SVZ-derived olfactory bulb interneurons, but final interneuron subtypes and developmental progression require further characterization.

name: Cerebral Cortex Layer II Immature Neuron
morphology_summary: Where reported (e.g., in degus), DCX-labeled immature neurons are predominantly located in layer II of neocortex, particularly in limbic cortices. Cells do not co-stain with NeuN, confirming immature status. No detailed dendritic/axonal maturation stages described.
ephys_summary: No electrophysiological characterization described in the report; presence and functional significance of cortical immature neurons remains controversial and species-dependent.
subtypes_noted: No subtypes defined. Presence of cortical immature neurons is conflicting across studies and species: Dayer et al. (2005) found no DCX+ cells in rodent neocortex, while Groen et al. (2021) reported widespread DCX+ layer II neurons in degu neocortex, concentrated in limbic cortices.
